item.title: UK government to overhaul ‘outdated’ home-selling process
item.description: Vendors will need to produce ‘sales packs’ containing information on the property’s condition prior to listing

item.title: Gulf oil shock speeds up India’s EV drive
item.description: Carmakers report increasing sales and showroom enquiries as fuel costs rise

item.title: Rape convictions under review after UK detective allegedly used AI chatbot for paperwork
item.description: Derbyshire Police investigating whether officer used software to secure desired court outcome

item.title: CME sues US derivatives regulator over crypto contract approval
item.description: Exchange operator says allowance of ‘perpetual’ futures contracts on digital currencies by Kalshi violates law

item.title: Robinhood is well placed for the casino-ification of everything
item.description: Robinhood is well placed for the casino-ification of everything【原文：Robinhood is well placed for the casino-ification of everything】

item.title: Is Tesla destined to be folded into SpaceX?
item.description: Wall Street simmers with the idea of a merger of Elon Musk’s companies

item.title: Jet fuel prices tumble on prospect of renewed Gulf exports
item.description: Airlines get relief from soaring fuel costs following deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Gatwick airport launches legal action after ministers relax runway slot rules
item.description: Regulation was intended to protect the airline industry from jet fuel shortages following the Iran crisis

item.title: US-Iran accord opens way for Hormuz charges, industry warns
item.description: Shipping executives fear language will enable Tehran to introduce fees after 60 days or a fund similar to Strait of Malacca

item.title: Trump is taking a page out of China’s sovereign AI playbook
item.description: Governments have long protected strategic industries — what is new is their willingness to become shareholders

item.title: VW supervisory board member exit a ‘very negative signal’ on corporate governance
item.description: Unexpected resignation of Susanne Wiegand deepens concerns among investors

item.title: Is the world becoming more predictable?
item.description: Bigger and better data sets and powerful AI models are allowing us to spot previously undetectable patterns

item.title: Accenture shares set to fall to lowest since 2017 as AI threat mounts
item.description: IT consultancy hit by concerns technology will hurt its business model

item.title: Saudi wealth fund warns European regulations ‘hurting’ foreign investors
item.description: Comments from Public Investment Fund governor come amid concerns about EU’s foreign subsidies regulation

item.title: How AI is ‘senior-ising’ junior roles
item.description: Changing workflows mean employers are now asking new recruits to be managers and decision makers

item.title: Hegseth unveils six-month review of US military presence in Europe
item.description: Defence secretary announced decision as he scolded Nato allies for failing to support Washington in Iran war

item.title: Nuclear watchdog chief pitches ‘leaner’ UN in secretary-general bid
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: crude stocks in US Strategic Petroleum Reserve fall to lowest level since 1983

item.title: Nissan in talks over more UK government help for Sunderland plant
item.description: Any deal is likely to depend on Britain watering down its electric vehicle targets

item.title: FirstFT: Warsh ushers in new Fed era
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US and Iran sign interim peace deal and JPMorgan bans workers in Hong Kong from using Claude

item.title: Better internet, more pay: will Hormuz crisis improve life at sea?
item.description: Months stuck on stranded vessels have exposed mental and physical strains on shipping crews

item.title: BHP takes $2.3bn writedown on Canadian fertiliser project
item.description: Australian miner’s hit on troubled Jansen development is last big act of Mike Henry’s tenure as chief

item.title: Aldi is making a $9bn bet that it can finally crack America
item.description: After 50 years of slow US expansion the German discount grocer is upping the pace. Can it win over hard-pressed consumers?

item.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with largest-ever drone attack
item.description: Nearly 200 drones strike Russian capital, several hitting the city’s largest oil refinery

item.title: How Uzbekistan could liberalise its economy with a push into green energy | FT Film
item.description: Will the former soviet state liberalise its economy with a push into green energy?

item.title: More than a million landlords unprepared for tougher energy efficiency rules
item.description: Buy-to-let lender to offer discounted loans to help borrowers fund improvements before rules tighten in 2030

item.title: Intertek board agrees £10bn takeover by EQT
item.description: Swedish private equity group is investing alongside Emirati sovereign investors ADIA and Mubadala

item.title: BBC cuts will damage broadcaster’s crown jewels
item.description: Government should consider direct licence fee increases or give corporation greater freedom to monetise its content

item.title: AI boosts Samsung but batters IT jobs
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Tesco’s UK sales growth slows despite rise in market share
item.description: Supermarket chain maintains cautious outlook amid conflict in Middle East

item.title: Octopus founder says climate policies must address energy costs
item.description: Remarks come ahead of pivotal English by-election
